summary: Created a minimal dependency-ordered M4 task DAG covering campaign objectives, muster/prep, mobilized commitments, grain supply, route/transport capacity, march execution, supply state, reinforcement timing, automatic engagement, siege choices, withdrawal, postwar handoff to M3 governance, minimal campaign AI, campaign planning UI, determinism/replay, stress validation, and M4 exit validation without implementing M4 systems.

tests_run.result: Modified planning/status files: PROJECT_STATUS.md, project/goal-mode-state.json, project/messages/outbox/GOAL-MODE-CONTINUATION.json, project/tasks/active/M4-TASK-GRAPH-001.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json. New M4 task files and systems handoff are untracked under allowed project/tasks/active and project/messages/outbox paths.

tests_run.command: node -e diff scope guard for tracked and untracked changed paths
tests_run.result: diff scope ok paths=18; no forbidden path patterns were touched.
Listed prior tasks as CLOSED; M4-TASK-GRAPH-001 as REVIEW; 12 downstream M4 tasks as DRAFT.
No output while M4-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON validation for all M4 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape
tests_run.result: M4 JSON DAG validation ok files=13 edges=27.

tests_run.command: node -e ASCII validation for new M4 JSON task and handoff files
tests_run.result: M4 new JSON ASCII check ok files=14.

tests_run.command: node -e taskctl validate for every M4 task file
tests_run.result: taskctl validate all M4 ok count=13.

decisions: All downstream M4 implementation and validation tasks remain DRAFT; no M4 implementation task was advanced to READY.

The M4 DAG follows a narrow dependency spine: campaign objectives/knowledge, muster commitments, grain supply, route/transport capacity, march/supply/reinforcement execution, automatic engagement/siege, withdrawal/postwar handoff, AI, client UI, determinism/replay, stress validation, and exit gate.
Campaign AI is scoped to existing sim-core/protocol boundaries because the repository currently has no separate sim-ai package; no new package or manager abstraction is introduced by the graph.
The M4 postwar result task must hand off to accepted M3 postwar governance paths and must not create a parallel settlement/diplomacy model.
M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001 covers deterministic replay and explicit M4 persistence/rejection boundaries so M4 state cannot be silently lost.
The DAG explicitly excludes manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
risks: M4 implementation tasks are mostly R3 because they touch deterministic authoritative simulation state, protocol payloads, route/supply accounting, automatic resolution, save/replay boundaries, or client command/read-model surfaces.
M4 route and river transport must stay a simple capacity model over accepted M2 scaffolds; any push toward complex naval warfare is out of scope and should stop.
M4 AI must remain minimal and explanatory. A general grand-strategy AI or hidden scoring manager would exceed this graph.
M4 stress validation is milestone evidence only, not final M5 player-study or final H-004 proof.
requested_action: Review the M4 DAG for Roadmap/acceptance-matrix completeness, dependency ordering, architecture-boundary discipline, explicit exclusions, downstream DRAFT status, and handoff validity. Accept or request changes.
